label: Grade 4

narrative.intro: Welcome to Barrigón! We are going on a walk through the neighborhood. What is your favorite place? 🏫🛒⛪🌊💧🌳

listening.instruction: 🎧 Listen carefully! What is Carlos's favorite place? Tap it!
listening.ttsText: Hi! My name is Carlos. I live in Barrigón. I love my neighborhood! There are many places here — the school, the shops, the church, the river, and the park. But my favorite place is the river. I love to go there on Thursdays. The river is beautiful and cool. This is my favorite place in Barrigón!
options: school 🏫
shops 🛒
river 🌊
park 🌳
listening.feedback: Yes! Carlos's favorite place is the river! 🌊

reading.story:
My name is Ana. I live in Barrigón.
I love my neighborhood! Every day I walk to school. 🏫
On Tuesdays, I go to the shops with my mom. 🛒
But my favorite place is Omar Torrijos Park. 🌳
It is green, big, and beautiful.
I go there every Sunday with my family.
reading.question: Why does Ana love Omar Torrijos Park?
options: Because it has a market.
Because it is green, big, and beautiful.
Because she goes there on Tuesdays.
Because her school is there.
reading.feedback: Great reading! The park is green, big, and beautiful! 🌳

writing.instruction: ✏️ Complete the sentence with TWO words!
writing.prompt:
My favorite place in Barrigón is the _______.
It is _______ and beautiful.
writing.hint: Think about a place on the map you love! 🗺️
writing.feedback: Great writing! My favorite place is beautiful! 🌟

speaking.instruction: 🗣️ Talk about YOUR favorite place in Barrigón!
speaking.prompt: My favorite place in Barrigón is the park. It is green and beautiful. I go there on Sundays!
speaking.ttsText: My favorite place in Barrigón is the park. It is green and beautiful. I go there on Sundays!
speaking.caption: Barrigón — Your Favorite Place!
speaking.feedback: Wonderful! You described your favorite place! 🌟

mediation.instruction: 🌐 A new student arrives. Help them understand!
mediation.prompt:
A new student from the city says:
"En mi ciudad, mi lugar favorito es el parque porque es grande y bonito."
options: In my city, my favorite place is the school because it is big.
In my city, my favorite place is the park because it is large and beautiful.
My city has a market and a river.
I don't have a favorite place in my city.
mediation.feedback: Excellent mediation! The park is large and beautiful! 🌳

narrative.intro: Barrigón is amazing! Let's tell visitors why our neighborhood is the BEST! 🏆

listening.instruction: 🎧 Listen! Which place does the guide recommend? Tap it!
listening.ttsText: Welcome to Barrigón! This neighborhood is the best! You must visit Las Yayas Waterfall — it is incredible and unique! The water is cold and crystal clear. It is the best place in all of Barrigón! Don't miss it!
options: The School 🏫
The Shops 🛒
Las Yayas Waterfall 💧
The Church ⛪
listening.feedback: Yes! Las Yayas Waterfall is the best! 💧🏆

reading.instruction: 📖 Read and choose the best answer!
reading.story:
Barrigón is the best neighborhood in Panama!
It has a beautiful school, colorful shops, and a historic church.
But the most amazing place is Omar Torrijos National Park. 🌳
The park has incredible trees, birds, and animals.
Visitors from all over Panama come to see it.
It is truly the best!
reading.question: Why do visitors come to Barrigón?
options: To buy things at the shops.
To study at the school.
To see Omar Torrijos National Park.
To swim in the river.
reading.feedback: Great! Visitors come to see Omar Torrijos Park! 🌳

writing.prompt:
You must visit _______!
It is the best place in Barrigón!
writing.hint: It has incredible trees and animals! 🌳

speaking.prompt: Barrigón is the best neighborhood! You must visit Las Yayas Waterfall — it's incredible and unique!
speaking.ttsText: Barrigón is the best neighborhood! You must visit Las Yayas Waterfall — it's incredible and unique!
speaking.caption: Las Yayas — It's the Best! 🏆

mediation.instruction: 🌐 A tourist needs help! What does the sign say?
mediation.prompt:
A tourist reads a sign:
"¡El Parque Omar Torrijos es lo mejor! ¡No te lo pierdas!"
options: Omar Torrijos Park is closed today.
Omar Torrijos Park is the best! Don't miss it!
Omar Torrijos Park is far from here.
Omar Torrijos Park has a restaurant.
mediation.feedback: Perfect! Omar Torrijos Park is the best! Don't miss it! 🌳🏆

narrative.intro: Near Omar Torrijos Park in Barrigón, families grow vegetables! Let's help in the garden! 🌱🌳

listening.instruction: 🎧 Listen! How do the tomatoes grow? Tap it!
listening.ttsText: Come to the garden near Omar Torrijos Park! Look at these tomatoes! They grow quickly in the Panamanian sun. We water them every day and they grow fast. The tomatoes grow quickly because they have good soil, water, and lots of sunshine!
options: slowly 🐢
quickly 🚀
never ❌
quietly 🤫

reading.instruction: 📖 Read and choose the best answer!
reading.story:
Near Omar Torrijos Park, we have a community garden. 🌱
We plant tomatoes, peppers, and corn.
The tomatoes grow quickly because Panama has lots of sun and rain.
We water the plants every morning.
In six weeks, we can harvest the tomatoes!
Fresh tomatoes are delicious and healthy.
reading.question: Why do the tomatoes grow quickly?
options: Because they are planted near the school.
Because Panama has lots of sun and rain.
Because the students water them at night.
Because tomatoes always grow fast.
reading.feedback: Excellent! Panama's sun and rain make them grow quickly! 🍅☀️🌧️

writing.prompt: The tomatoes grow _______ because Panama has lots of sun and _______.

speaking.prompt: The tomatoes grow quickly! We water them every day. Panama has lots of sun and rain!
speaking.ttsText: The tomatoes grow quickly! We water them every day. Panama has lots of sun and rain!
speaking.caption: Community Garden — near Omar Torrijos Park 🌳

mediation.instruction: 🌐 A friend from another country asks about your garden!
mediation.prompt:
Your friend asks:
"¿Por qué los tomates crecen tan rápido en Panamá?"
options: Because we plant them near the river.
Because Panamanian tomatoes are special.
Because Panama has lots of sun and rain.
Because we harvest them every day.
mediation.feedback: Great mediation! Panama has lots of sun and rain! ☀️🌧️🍅

narrative.intro: Omar Torrijos Park has amazing native plants from Panama! Let's learn about them! 🌿🌳

listening.instruction: 🎧 Listen! Where are these plants from? Tap it!
listening.ttsText: Look at these beautiful plants! These plants are from Panama. They are native to our country. You can find them in Omar Torrijos National Park right here in Barrigón. We must protect them because they are special and unique to Panama!
options: Mexico 🇲🇽
Colombia 🇨🇴
Panama 🇵🇦
Costa Rica 🇨🇷
listening.feedback: Yes! These plants are from Panama! 🌿🇵🇦

reading.story:
Omar Torrijos National Park is home to many native plants. 🌿
These plants are from Panama — they are endemic species.
You cannot find them anywhere else in the world!
The tropical climate of Panama helps them grow.
It is our responsibility to protect these amazing plants.
They are part of our natural heritage.
reading.question: What makes Panama's endemic plants special?
options: They grow very quickly.
They are very colorful.
You cannot find them anywhere else in the world.
They only grow near the river.
reading.feedback: Excellent! Panama's plants are unique in the world! 🌿🌍

writing.prompt:
These plants are from _______.
We must _______ them!
writing.hint: They are native to our country! 🌿🇵🇦
writing.feedback: Perfect! These plants are from Panama and we must protect them! 🌿

speaking.instruction: 🗣️ Tell a visitor about Panama's plants!
speaking.prompt: These plants are from Panama! They are native and special. We must protect them!
speaking.ttsText: These plants are from Panama! They are native and special. We must protect them!
speaking.caption: Omar Torrijos Park 🌳 — Native Plants

mediation.instruction: 🌐 A tourist asks about the plants in the park!
mediation.prompt:
A tourist says:
"¿Estas plantas son de Panamá? ¿Son especiales?"
options: Yes! These plants are from Colombia and are very common.
Yes! These plants are from Panama and are endemic — very special and unique!
No, these plants are from all over the world.
Yes, but we don't need to protect them.
mediation.feedback: Perfect mediation! Panama's plants are endemic and unique! 🌿🇵🇦

themes.title: The Panama Canal Is 110 Years Old.
We Recycle Plastic Bottles Every Day.
Turn Off the Lights at Night.
We Wear a Pollera at Festivals.
